EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms moved across southern Mississippi flooding numerous roads and homes. A few roads were closed and a few bridges were washed out. Several businesses were flooded as well. In Hattiesburg, nearly ten inches of rain fell flooding places that have never been flooded before. Homes had to be evacuated in Hattiesburg. Forrest and Lamar Counties received most of the flood related damage. One person died when their vehicle was swept off of a bridge into a flood swollen creek. The driver of the vehicle could not swim to safety and rescue attempts failed.

Strong thunderstorms moved across southern Mississippi flooding numerous roads and homes. A few roads were closed and a few bridges were washed out. Several businesses were flooded as well. Forrest and Lamar Counties received most of the flood related damage. One person died when their vehicle was swept off of a bridge into a flood swollen creek. The driver of the vehicle could not swim to safety and rescue attempts failed.

This tornado touched down in open field and moved northeast towards Blaine. A few trees and power lines were blown down until it reached Blaine. At Blaine, the storm did substantial damage to four wood structured houses and one brick structured house. One mobile home was destroyed and another received heavy damage.  Two automobiles were also damaged by the debris. Further northeast along the track, the tornado destroyed two wood framed churches near Doddsville. Also a pivot irrigation system was over-turned and several power lines and power poles were blown down.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms moved across southern Mississippi flooding numerous roads and homes. A few roads were closed and a few bridges were washed out. Several businesses were flooded as well. In Hattiesburg, nearly ten inches of rain fell flooding places that have never been flooded before. Homes had to be evacuated in Hattiesburg. Forrest and Lamar Counties received most of the flood related damage. One person died when their vehicle was swept off of a bridge into a flood swollen creek. The driver of the vehicle could not swim to safety and rescue attempts failed.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Golfball size hail damaged the roofs of several houses and damaged several automobiles.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: This weak tornado was tracked by emergency management officials across Bolivar and Sunflower Counties. The tornado moved across open areas and no damage was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm wind gusts blew plate glass windows out of a restaurant just east of Picayune. Two people suffered minor injuries from flying glass. In addition,  the roofs of small shopping mall and a church in Picayune were damaged by thunderstorm wind gusts. Dime size hail was also reported with the severe thunderstorm.
Sheriffs office reported thunderstorm wind gusts blew  several cars into each other and eventually off of Interstate Highway 10.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: One home had several shingles blown off of its roof.
A 4,000-seat gym and auditorium on the campus of Coahoma Community College in Clarksdale was damaged by high winds.
Heavy rains produced flash flooding in the southern part of Calhoun City. One home was surrounded by water.
A few trees were blown down.
